segments.p1-6-g11: The noun 'Ответ' (nominative singular) is used as a label/heading to mark the reply to the preceding question; the colon shows it introduces the answer that follows.
segments.p1-6-g12: A single noun in the nominative, 'лентяй' supplies the content of the answer—'an idler'—and the semicolon connects this blunt label to the following comment.
segments.p1-6-g13: 'да ведь' is an emphatic/discourse particle cluster (here roughly 'well/after all') introducing the speaker's reaction. 'это' is an anticipatory/dummy subject ('it/this'), and 'преприятно' is an impersonal predicative (neuter) meaning 'very/most pleasant' (the prefix пре- intensifies the adjective). The combination 'было бы' is the past-tense form plus the conditional particle 'бы', used to form a hypothetical conditional ('would be'). The infinitive 'слышать' is the notional verb complement ('to hear' / 'to be told'), and 'о себе' is the prepositional phrase 'about oneself' that completes the idea: literally, 'It would have been very pleasant to hear that about oneself.' Word order here is colloquial and slightly inverted — the impersonal predicate precedes the infinitive clause it comments on.
segments.p1-6-g14: Значит is a discourse particle meaning "so/that means/therefore," used to draw an inference from what preceded. It stands alone here as a connective introductory word leading into the clause.
segments.p1-6-g15: положительно is an adverb "positively/definitely" modifying the short passive participle определен (определён). определен is the short form passive participle/predicate (masculine singular) meaning "determined/defined/settled" — the subject is omitted and understood from context.
segments.p1-6-g16: Another occurrence of the particle значит, functioning the same way as the first: it signals a conclusion drawn from the previous clause ("that means/so"). It introduces the consequence clause that follows.
segments.p1-6-g17: есть here is the existential/impersonal use of "to be" (literally "there is"). что with an infinitive (сказать) functions as an indefinite pronoun meaning "something (to)" — so есть что сказать = "there is something to say." обо мне is the prepositional phrase "about me."
segments.p1-6-g18: Лентяй is a noun used as an exclamatory address or interjection here (equivalent to calling someone an "idler" or "sluggard"). In Russian the form is nominative and functions as a shout/label rather than a grammatically marked vocative.
segments.p1-6-g19: да is a colloquial particle here (lit. "yes") serving as a discourse filler/softener; ведь adds emphasis meaning "after all/you know." это is the demonstrative "this/it is." званье and назначенье are older/colloquial spellings of звание and назначение meaning "title/designation" and "appointment/calling" respectively; the phrase states that the label is an official designation or calling.
segments.p1-6-g20: эта(то) это again is the demonstrative "this/it is," and карьера is the noun "career." The final "-с" attached to карьера (карьера-с) is a colloquial, stylistic clipping/afterthought used in 19th-century Russian to give a conversational or ironic cadence (similar to adding a trailing "—so" or a clipped colloquial tag in English).
segments.p1-6-g78: А is a short discourse particle here (roughly 'well' or 'but') used to catch attention; ведь is an emphasizer meaning 'after all' that strengthens what follows. Как хотите is an idiomatic concessive/conditional phrase meaning 'as you like' or 'if you wish'; хотИте is 2nd person plural present indicative addressing the (plural) audience, so the whole segment functions as a concessive lead-in rather than a full clause.
segments.p1-6-g79: Такие отзывы is the noun phrase 'such responses/remarks' (taкие modifies отзывы). Преприятно слышать is an impersonal construction: преприятно ('very pleasant', here an emphatic/adverbial adjective with the intensifying prefix пре-) plus the infinitive слышать ('to hear'), so literally 'it is very pleasant to hear such responses'. В наш отрицательный век is a prepositional/time-or-era phrase ('in our negative age') modifying the whole predicate; наш is a possessive pronoun agreeing with век.
segments.p1-6-g80: Господа is a direct address in the plural (vocative/nominal form used to address an audience: 'gentlemen'/'sirs'). It stands alone as an addressee tag and does not change the sentence's grammatical structure.
segments.p1-6-g29: Он умер is the simple past statement 'he died' (умер is the past tense of умереть). The phrase не то что is an idiomatic contrast marker here (= 'not so much ... as ...'); с покойной uses the preposition с + instrumental (покойной) where покойной is used substantively ('the peaceful one') — the noun it stands for (совестью) is omitted and understood from the context.
segments.p1-6-g30: А is a contrastive conjunction ('but/rather'); с торжествующей совестью is again с + instrumental (совестью) meaning 'with a triumphant conscience'. Торжествующей is the long form of the present active participle/adjectival form agreeing with совестью in case and number and describes the state of the conscience.
segments.p1-6-g31: и был совершенно прав: был is the past-tense copula ('he was') and with the short adjective прав functions as a predicative ('was right'). Совершенно is an adverb modifying прав ('completely/entirely right'). The whole clause affirms the correctness of the previous contrast.
segments.p1-6-g32: А introduces a contrastive remark ('as for me/and'). Я бы выбрал is the conditional formed with the past tense verb (выбрал) plus the particle бы ('I would have chosen'); себе is a dative reflexive meaning 'for myself' (indicating benefiting/distribution), тогда is a temporal adverb 'then', and карьеру is the direct object in accusative ('career').
segments.p1-6-g33: а был бы лентяй и обжора repeats the conditional ('I would have been') with был бы (past + бы = 'would have been'). Лентяй and обжора are predicate nouns (nominative) joined by и — two qualities the speaker imagines having (an idler and a glutton).
segments.p1-6-g34: но не простой, negates 'simple/ordinary' (не + adjective) and contrasts the previous nominal predicates: 'but not a simple/ordinary one.' Here простой is a predicate adjective describing the kind of lout/glutton he would be (negated).
segments.p1-6-g35: а is a short contrastive particle linking the negation to the reproposal that follows ('but rather'). It signals correction or specification.
segments.p1-6-g36: например is a parenthetical adverb 'for example', marking the upcoming specification as an instance of the preceding contrast.
segments.p1-6-g37: сочувствующий is the present active participle/adjectival form meaning 'sympathizing' or 'sympathetic' and functions as an adjective modifying the (implied) person; сочувствоватьgov ernment requires a dative object, so всему (dative of весь) is 'to all/everything'. Прекрасному and высокому are dative singular adjectives agreeing with всему and form the phrase 'to all that is beautiful and lofty.'
segments.p1-6-g38: 'Как' is an interrogative adverb meaning 'how' and introduces the question. 'Вам' is the dative form of 'вы' marking the experiencer ('to/for you'); with 'нравиться' the thing that pleases is grammatically the subject in nominative. 'Это' is the thing that (literally) 'pleases' and occupies the foreground position. 'Нравится' is 3rd person singular present of 'нравиться' and here forms the standard dative-experiencer construction meaning 'to please / be liked by'. Word order is neutral but topicalizes 'это' (this) before the verb.
segments.p1-6-g39: 'Мне' is dative and marks the experiencer ('to me'); in impersonal or perception verbs (like 'мерещиться') the experiencer is dative. 'Это' is the notional subject/thing that appeared in the speaker's mind. 'Давно' is an adverb meaning 'for a long time' (duration) and modifies the verb. 'Мерещилось' is the past neuter form of the reflexive/impersonal verb 'мерещиться' ('to seem/appear, often of an illusion'); used impersonally with a dative experiencer it means 'it seemed to me / I had the impression', here with 'давно' giving the sense 'I had long imagined/seen it.'
segments.p1-6-g64: Literal order is inverted: the object phrase Уважения к себе за это ("respect toward myself for this") comes before the verb потребую. Потребую is first-person singular (present form used with a future/intentional force here) meaning "I will demand" or "I demand." К + dative себе marks the target/recipient of the respect, and за это = "on account of this."
segments.p1-6-g65: The structure преследовать буду uses the infinitive + auxiliary буду to form the future (infinitive before the auxiliary for emphasis/colloquial style). Того is the demonstrative/indefinite pronoun "that one/the one" as the object of преследовать ("to pursue").
segments.p1-6-g66: A relative clause introduced by кто ("who/whoever") follows. Не будет оказывать is a periphrastic future negative (будет + imperfective infinitive), meaning "will not show"; мне is dative marking the person to whom the action is directed, and уважения is the noun object (here "respect").
segments.p1-6-g67: Живу is first-person singular present ("I live/I lead my life"); спокойно is an adverb of manner placed after the verb and means "calmly/quietly." The short clause states a habitual or characteristic way of living.
segments.p1-6-g68: Умираю is first-person present ("I die/I am dying") used hyperbolically here; торжественно is an adverb meaning "solemnly/triumpantly." Together they contrast the manner of living and dying.
segments.p1-6-g69: Да ведь is a colloquial intensifier (да as an interjection/particle plus ведь "after all/you know") introducing the exclamation. Это прелесть is a simple nominal predication: это = "this/it" and прелесть = "a delight/charm," i.e. "it's a delight."
segments.p1-6-g70: Целая is an attributive adjective here used emphatically (literally "whole/entire") to strengthen прелесть; the phrase целая прелесть is idiomatic for "an absolute/complete delight."
segments.p1-6-g71: 'И' is the coordinating conjunction 'and'. 'Такое' is a demonstrative/intensifying pronoun 'such (a)'; placed before the verb it emphasizes the quality of what is grown. 'Себе' is a dative reflexive used colloquially to mean 'for myself' or 'on myself' (emphatic). 'Отрастил' is past tense (masculine) of 'отрастить' (to grow something) and with the particle 'бы' (conditional) yields the counterfactual 'would have grown'. 'Я' is the subject; 'тогда' 'then/in that case'; 'брюхо' is the direct object (accusative) 'belly'. Note the conditional is formed by past tense + 'бы' and the word order is somewhat colloquial/folksy.
segments.p1-6-g72: 'Такой тройной подбородок' is a noun phrase 'such a triple chin' functioning as the object; 'такой' intensifies. 'Соорудил' is past tense of 'соорудить' (to construct/make) and continues the hypothetical series of actions; the conditional sense carries over from the earlier 'бы' (it needn't be repeated to apply). The implied subject is still 'я'.
segments.p1-6-g73: 'Такой бы' again emphasizes with the conditional particle 'бы' explicitly present for emphasis. 'Сандальный нос' literally 'a sandal-like nose' (a mocking, descriptive compound). 'Себе' marks that this feature is produced for himself (dative reflexive). 'Выработал' is past tense of 'выработать' used here in the sense 'to develop/produce/shape' (i.e., to work out or fashion for oneself). The clause is parallel to the previous ones and expresses another hypothetical alteration of his appearance.
segments.p1-6-g74: 'Что' introduces the result/consequence clause 'so that/that'. 'Всякий встречный' is an idiom meaning 'every passerby / anyone he met' (subject, nominative). 'Сказал бы' is past-tense verb + conditional particle 'бы' meaning 'would have said' (conditional), with 'бы' after the verb in colloquial speech.
segments.p1-6-g75: 'Смотря' is the adverbial present participle (деепричастие) of 'смотреть' meaning 'looking' or 'as he looked'; it forms an adverbial clause 'looking at me' that describes the manner in which the passerby would speak. 'На' is the preposition used with 'смотреть' meaning 'at/toward', and 'меня' is the accusative 'me'.
segments.p1-6-g76: The exclamation 'Вот так плюс!' is colloquial: 'Вот так' ('now that's something' / 'what a ...') and 'плюс' ('a plus' / 'an advantage' / 'a positive'), used together as an approving or astonished exclamation ('Now that's a plus!'). The phrase is emphatic and idiomatic.
segments.p1-6-g77: 'вот так' is an exclamative, fronted for emphasis meaning roughly 'now/that's how' or 'that’s a case of'; 'уж' here is a forceful particle (not the temporal 'already') that intensifies the statement. 'настоящее положительное' is a noun phrase with 'положительное' used as a nominalized neuter adjective ('a positive thing') and 'настоящее' agreeing with it in gender/number to mean 'real/genuine'; there is no verb — the whole line is an emphatic exclamation.
segments.p1-6-g1: 'О' here is an exclamatory interjection (vocative-like) expressing emotion or regret; it stands alone before the conditional clause and does not literally mean 'about'.
segments.p1-6-g2: This is a conditional clause: 'если (бы)' is the conditional marker ('if' / 'if only'), with 'бы' attached to form the subjunctive. 'я ничего не делал' uses the past imperfective 'делал' plus negation 'ничего не' to mean 'did nothing at all' (a hypothetical past used for counterfactuals). 'только из лени' is an adverbial phrase of motive: 'only/merely out of laziness' (literally 'from laziness').
segments.p1-6-g3: 'Господи' is a vocative/exclamatory form addressing God or used as an interjection ('Lord' / 'My God') and sets an emotional tone for the following hypothetical statement.
segments.p1-6-g4: 'как бы' + past tense with 'бы' expresses an imagined degree: 'how I would...'. 'тогда' places the hypothetical in that imagined circumstance ('then' / 'in that case'). 'себя уважал' is reflexive object + past imperfective 'уважал' used with 'бы' to form the conditional 'would respect myself'. Word order is standard (subject 'я' before reflexive 'себя' and verb).
segments.p1-6-g5: 'Уважал бы' is the conditional form ('would respect') formed by the past tense 'уважал' plus particle 'бы'. 'именно' is an intensifier 'precisely' and 'потому' begins the causal explanation ('for that reason' / 'because'). This segment introduces a motive clause that follows.
segments.p1-6-g6: The clause begins with the conjunction 'что' ('that') introducing the explanation. 'хоть' here functions as 'at least' / 'even' (not 'although') and fronting the noun 'лень' gives emphasis: 'that at least laziness I would be in a position to have inside me.' 'в состоянии иметь' is a fixed construction meaning 'be able/capable of having', and 'в себе' means 'within myself'.
segments.p1-6-g7: Another concessive/limiting 'хоть' ('at least' / 'even if') introduces 'одно свойство было бы' — 'there would be at least one quality' — with 'было бы' forming a hypothetical/existential conditional. 'во мне' ('in me') locates that quality internally. 'как будто' ('as if' / 'as though') introduces a qualifying simile, 'и' here intensifies ('even' / 'also'), and 'положительное' is used as a noun meaning 'something positive'.
segments.p1-6-g8: 'в котором' is a relative pronoun phrase ('in which') referring back to 'свойство'. The clause 'я бы и сам был уверен' is conditional: 'I myself would be sure/confident' — 'и сам' adds emphasis ('even I myself' / 'I personally'), 'бы' marks the conditional, and 'был уверен' is the past participial construction used to make the conditional state.
segments.p1-6-g9: 'Вопрос:' is used as a label or introductory noun ('Question:') to introduce the following rhetorical question; it's tersely punctuated as a heading.
segments.p1-6-g10: 'кто' is the interrogative pronoun 'who'; 'такой' is a demonstrative meaning 'such/that' and here after 'кто' yields the idiomatic 'кто такой?' = 'who is that?' or 'what kind of person is this?'. The order is the standard interrogative pairing.
segments.p1-6-g21: "Не шутите" is a negative imperative addressed to you (plural or polite singular). The particle не + verb gives a prohibition; the form "шутите" is identical to the present indicative 2nd person plural but here functions as the imperative form (common in Russian). The comma marks a pause before the following assertion.
segments.p1-6-g22: "это так" is a short predicative clause where "это" is a demonstrative pronoun (subject/pointing word) and "так" is a predicative/adverb meaning "so/thus"; together they mean "that's so/it's true." Word order is subject + predicate; the copula is omitted because Russian often uses a bare predicate in such clauses.
segments.p1-6-g23: This clause omits the copula "(был)" so the sequence "Я тогда член..." is an elliptic nominal sentence meaning "I was then a member..." "самого" intensifies the superlative "первейшего" ("the very foremost"), and "по праву" is a set adverbial ("by right" or "rightly") formed with the preposition "по" + dative "праву." The coordinating "и занимаюсь" shifts to a present-tense verb (habitual present) meaning "and I occupy myself / I am engaged in." "занимаюсь только тем" sets up an object expressed by the following relative clause introduced by "тем, что..."
segments.p1-6-g24: The relative clause introduced by "что" contains the finite present verb "уважаю" (1st person singular): "that I constantly respect myself." "беспрерывно" (an adverb) modifies the verb and is fronted for emphasis; "себя" is the reflexive/object pronoun referring back to the subject "Я." The whole clause fills the object slot of "занимаюсь только тем, что..." ("I occupy myself only with the fact that...").
segments.p1-6-g25: "Я знал господина" uses the past tense verb "знал" (I knew) with the direct object "господина" (accusative), meaning "I was acquainted with / I knew a gentleman." The simple past places the acquaintance in the narrator's past experience.
segments.p1-6-g26: This relative clause is introduced by "который" (who) and contains the temporal adverbial "всю жизнь" (throughout his life). The reflexive verb "гордился" (was proud) governs the pronoun phrase "тем" which anticipates the following "что"-clause ("proud of the fact that..."). The structure expresses a sustained personal pride across the subject's life.
segments.p1-6-g27: "что знал толк в лафите" is a content clause completing "гордился тем." The idiom "знал толк в X" means "knew the value/was knowledgeable about X;" here "знал" is past-tense knowledge/skill and "толк" is the noun meaning "grasp/knack." The preposition "в" with the prepositional "лафите" names the domain (Lafite, the wine/brand).
segments.p1-6-g28: The clause uses past-tense narration: "Он считал" (he considered) with the construction "считать ... за ..." (= "to consider/regard ... as ..."). "это" is the object "that/this," "за" introduces the role/label ("as"), and the noun phrase "положительное свое достоинство" means "a positive merit/quality of his," with the possessive "свое" agreeing with the subject. The second coordinated clause "и никогда не сомневался в себе" uses the past imperfective "сомневался" (he did not have doubts) with the negator "не" and adverb "никогда" (never); "в себе" is a reflexive prepositional phrase meaning "in himself" (i.e., in his own worth/confidence).
segments.p1-6-g53: Я is the nominative subject 'I'. Сделался (from сделаться) is a past tense, perfective, reflexive verb meaning 'to become'; with the particle бы it forms a conditional 'would become'. Слезоточив is a short-form adjective/predicate meaning 'tearful' or 'producing tears' and follows the verb as the predicative complement.
segments.p1-6-g54: Как introduces a simile 'like/as'. Мокрая is a nominative feminine singular adjective 'wet' agreeing with губка, which is a nominative noun 'sponge' — the whole phrase compares the speaker's tearfulness to a wet sponge.
segments.p1-6-g55: Художник is a nominative noun 'artist' serving as the sentence subject; the comma after it marks a slight pause before the parenthetical adverb.
segments.p1-6-g56: Например is a parenthetical adverb 'for example', inserted to qualify the statement; it does not affect the core subject-verb agreement.
segments.p1-6-g57: Написал is past tense, perfective, 'wrote'/'painted' here meaning 'completed (a work)'. Картину is the direct object in the accusative 'a picture/painting'. Ге is the painter's name identifying the author of the painting; in this terse construction the name follows the object to indicate 'a picture by Ge' (the quoted senses list doesn't include a proper-name sense, so the appropriate sense is not listed).
segments.p1-6-g58: Тотчас же is an adverbial phrase meaning 'immediately', with же as an emphatic particle. Пью is present tense 'I drink' and here is used idiomatically to mean 'I make a toast'; за + здоровье forms the prepositional phrase 'to the health of', followed by художника in the genitive indicating whose health is toasted.
segments.p1-6-g59: Написавшего is a past active participle in the genitive masculine singular, agreeing with художника and functioning as a relative clause 'who wrote/painted'. Картину is the accusative object of that participle ('the picture'), and Ге again names the painter of the picture; the participle form here expresses 'who painted' (or 'who had painted') and is linked to художника by case agreement.
segments.p1-6-g60: Потому что is the subordinating conjunction 'because'. Люблю is present 'I love'; всё is a quantifier 'all/everything' modifying the nominalized adjectives прекрасное and высокое, which are neuter singular forms functioning as abstract nouns 'the beautiful' and 'the lofty' (i.e. 'all that is beautiful and lofty').
segments.p1-6-g61: Автор is the nominative subject 'the author'. Написал here means 'wrote' in the literal sense (he put the words down), and the quoted phrase как кому угодно is the direct object — an idiomatic expression meaning 'as one pleases/however anyone likes', where кому is dative indefinite and угодно is a short-form adjective meaning 'pleasing/acceptable'.
segments.p1-6-g62: Тотчас же пью за здоровье repeats the earlier structure 'immediately I drink/toast to the health of'. The quoted кого угодно ('whomever/anyone') is inserted as the object of the toast; кого here is an indefinite pronoun in the accusative meaning 'whomever', and угодно again means 'acceptable/any'. The whole phrase is somewhat ironical: the speaker toasts to 'anyone' because of his love of the beautiful.
segments.p1-6-g63: Потому что introduces the reason 'because'. Люблю is 'I love', всё is 'everything/all', and the quoted "прекрасное и высокое" uses nominalized adjectives (neuter) meaning 'the beautiful and the lofty' — again 'all that is beautiful and lofty'. This repeats the earlier abstract construction.
segments.p1-6-g40: Это + quoted nominalized adjectives acts as the subject: "This (the phrase) ‘the beautiful and lofty’". Сильно-таки is an adverb (сильно) with the colloquial/emphatic particle таки immediately after it, intensifying the action. Задавило is a perfective past verb (completed action) and takes мне in the dative as the experiencer and затылок as the direct object (form identical to nominative). В мои сорок лет is a temporal phrase with в + accusative indicating 'at the age of forty.'
segments.p1-6-g41: Но introduces a contrast with the preceding clause. Это here is a demonstrative referring back to the same thing; в мои сорок лет repeats the temporal expression (в + accusative) meaning 'at the age of forty.' The clause is elliptical (the verb from the previous clause is left understood) and functions as a corrective/time marker.
segments.p1-6-g42: А then introduces a contrasting turn toward the hypothetical past; тогда is an adverb 'then.' О is not the preposition but an exclamatory interjection 'oh!' used to heighten emotion — here it stands alone as an interjection before the following exclamation.
segments.p1-6-g43: Тогда было бы иначе is a full conditional: было (past tense of быть) + the conditional particle бы yields the English 'would have been' or 'would have been different.' Иначе is the adverb 'otherwise/differently.' Word order places тогда first for emphasis ('then it would have been different').
segments.p1-6-g44: Я бы + past tense отыскал forms a conditional/hypothetical ('I would have found'). Тотчас же is an adverbial phrase 'immediately' with же emphasizing immediacy. Себе is the dative reflexive indicating 'for myself.' Соответствующую деятельность is the direct object (accusative feminine singular) where the adjective agrees with деятельность.
segments.p1-6-g45: А именно is a fixed phrase introducing a specification or example: 'namely' or 'that is to say.' Here а is a simple connective and именно marks the coming clarification.
segments.p1-6-g46: Пить is an infinitive naming the activity ('to drink' / 'to toast'); за + здоровье forms the conventional toast construction 'to the health of' (за + accusative). Всего прекрасного и высокого uses the genitive of nominalized adjectives ('of all that is beautiful and lofty') — всегo is a genitive intensifier 'all of.'
segments.p1-6-g47: Я бы придирался uses the imperfective past + бы to form a habitual/iterative conditional ('I would be nitpicking' / 'I would pick at'). Ко is the allomorph of к used before certain consonants and governs the dative; ко всякому случаю = 'at every occasion' or 'on every pretext.'
segments.p1-6-g48: Чтоб (colloquial form of чтобы) introduces a purpose clause 'so that / in order that.' Сначала 'first' modifies the action. Пролить is perfective infinitive 'to shed/spill' (here 'to shed' a tear); в + accusative свой бокал indicates direction into 'my glass.' Слезу is the direct object (accusative singular).
segments.p1-6-g49: А потом is a connective sequence 'and then.' Выпить (perfective infinitive) + его ('it', accusative; here referring to the glass) means 'to drink it up.' За + noun (here omitted but understood as 'in honor of') functions as the toast marker 'for/to the health of'; все прекрасное и высокое is again a nominalized genitive phrase 'all that is beautiful and lofty.'
segments.p1-6-g50: Я бы + обратил is conditional (past form + бы) meaning 'I would (have) turned/converted.' Всё на свете is an idiomatic expression 'everything in the world' (на свете = 'in the world'). В + accusative (here 'в прекрасное') expresses conversion 'into the beautiful and lofty.' The adjective-nominals take accusative as the target of transformation.
segments.p1-6-g51: В here is the preposition 'in.' Гадчайшей is a superlative adjective in the prepositional case (feminine) 'the vilest' and is split off for emphasis; it will be completed by the noun in the next segment. This prepositional adjective is part of the locative phrase 'in the vilest ...'.
segments.p1-6-g52: Бесспорной дряни: дряни is the prepositional (or colloquial locative) form of дрянь 'filth/rubbish' and бесспорной ('indisputable') agrees with it — together 'in the indisputable filth.' Отыскал бы is past tense + бы for conditional 'would have found.' Прекрасное и высокое are the direct objects (nominalized adjectives) of отыскал (what he would have found even in filth).

sentences.p1-6-s5: Answer: an idler; well, it would be most pleasant to hear that about oneself.
sentences.p1-6-s6: So — positively determined; that means there is something to be said about me.
sentences.p1-6-s8: — But after all, that's a title and a calling; it's a career.
sentences.p1-6-s29: After all, if you like, it's very pleasant to hear such responses in our negative age, gentlemen.
sentences.p1-6-s13: He died not with a peaceful one, but with a triumphant conscience, and he was entirely right.
sentences.p1-6-s14: As for me, I would then have chosen a career for myself: I would have been a lazy idler and a glutton, but not an ordinary one — rather, for example, sympathetic to all that is beautiful and lofty.
sentences.p1-6-s25: I shall demand respect for myself on that account; I will pursue anyone who does not show me respect.
sentences.p1-6-s26: I live calmly, I die solemnly — why, it's a delight, an absolute delight!
sentences.p1-6-s27: And I'd have grown such a belly then, built such a triple chin, fashioned such a sandal-like nose for myself, that any passerby, looking at me, would have said, "Now that's a plus!"
sentences.p1-6-s1: Oh, if only I did nothing at all, merely out of laziness.
sentences.p1-6-s2: Lord, how I would then respect myself.
sentences.p1-6-s3: I would respect myself precisely because, at least, I would be capable of having laziness in me; at least one quality would be in me that seemed positive, in which I myself would be confident.
sentences.p1-6-s10: Then I was, by right, a member of the very foremost club, and I busied myself only with constantly respecting myself.
sentences.p1-6-s11: I knew a gentleman who took pride throughout his life in knowing a thing or two about Lafite.
sentences.p1-6-s12: He regarded that as a positive quality of his and never doubted himself.
sentences.p1-6-s21: I would become tearful, like a wet sponge.
sentences.p1-6-s22: An artist, for example, painted a picture by Ge.
sentences.p1-6-s23: Immediately I drink a toast to the health of the artist who painted Ge's picture, because I love all that is beautiful and lofty.
sentences.p1-6-s24: The author wrote 'as one pleases'; immediately I drink to the health of 'whomever', because I love all that is 'beautiful and lofty'.
sentences.p1-6-s17: That "beautiful and lofty" pressed heavily on the back of my head when I was forty; but that was at forty, and then — oh, then it would have been different!
sentences.p1-6-s18: I would at once have found for myself a suitable activity — namely: to drink to the health of all that is beautiful and lofty.
sentences.p1-6-s19: I would pick at every occasion so as first to spill a tear into my glass, and then to drink it for all that is beautiful and lofty.
sentences.p1-6-s20: I would then have turned everything in the world into the beautiful and lofty; in the vilest, indisputable filth I would have found the beautiful and lofty.
